questions.scenario: Cloud Run fails with `Permission 'secretmanager.versions.access' denied` on `projects/devpolaris-orders-prod/secrets/orders-db-url`. What is the best first framing?

options.text: Rebuild the access sentence: which principal needs which role on which resource.
Grant project Owner immediately because the error mentions production.
Move the secret into the container image so IAM is not involved.
Delete the secret because denied requests prove it is unused.

questions.explanation: The article teaches IAM as a relationship. The team should identify principal, permission, resource, role, binding, and scope before changing access.

questions.scenario: The request was made by `serviceAccount:orders-api-prod@devpolaris-orders-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com`. In the IAM sentence, what is this?
questions.explanation: A service account is a principal: the actor making the request.

questions.scenario: Which pieces belong in the access sentence review?

options.text: Exact resource and scope of the binding.
A broad role chosen before reading the error.

questions.explanation: A useful review starts from evidence. A broad role chosen before reading the sentence is a guess.
